user_query:
I need an end-to-end ASO overhaul for our nutrition and meal-planning app. Please read these inputs: - input/app_info.json (brand, audience, features, current baseline metadata) - input/seed_keywords.csv (seed keywords with rough volume and competition estimates) - input/competitors.txt (10 competitor app names). Then produce the following deliverables and save them under output/:

1) output/keyword_research.json — A prioritized list of at least 25 keywords with scoring and placement mapping. For each keyword, include: keyword, relevance (0-100), volume (estimated monthly searches), competition (0-100, lower is easier), conversion_intent (0-100), overall_score (0-100), and placement (one of: "title", "subtitle", "keyword_field_ios", "short_description", "full_description"). Do not include any competitor brand names from input/competitors.txt. Ensure you explicitly designate which single keyword is the primary keyword (max overall_score) to use in titles.

2) output/metadata_ios.json — Optimized iOS metadata as a JSON object with fields: title, subtitle, keywords_field, promotional_text (optional). Constraints: title ≤ 30 chars and must begin with the brand from input/app_info.json and include the primary keyword; subtitle ≤ 30 chars and include a secondary keyword; keywords_field ≤ 100 chars, comma-separated with no spaces after commas, no words duplicated from the title, and no competitor brand names. Keep language natural (no stuffing).

3) output/metadata_android.json — Optimized Google Play metadata as a JSON object with fields: title, short_description, full_description. Constraints: title ≤ 50 chars and must include the brand and the primary keyword; short_description ≤ 80 chars and clearly state a user benefit with an action verb; full_description 500–2,000 characters, structured with a hook paragraph, feature bullets, a brief social proof section, and a call to action, naturally integrating the primary and secondary keywords without exceeding ~5% density for any single term.

4) output/competitor_matrix.csv — A keyword vs competitor coverage matrix. Use a header with "keyword" followed by each competitor name exactly as listed in input/competitors.txt (10 columns for competitors + the keyword column). Include at least 15 keywords as rows. Mark coverage as 1 if that competitor appears to target the keyword (e.g., in title/short description) and 0 otherwise.

5) output/ab_test_plan.md — A concise A/B test plan prioritizing tests for (a) App Icon and (b) Title. Include clear HYPOTHESIS lines for each, expected lift, 95% significance, required sample size per variant (based on a reasonable baseline CVR assumption) and recommended minimum duration (≥7 days). Make the plan actionable.

6) output/aso_health_score.json — A JSON object with: overall (0–100) and categories with exactly four keys: Metadata, Ratings, Keywords, Conversion (each 0–25). Briefly note 3–5 prioritized recommendations in a "recommendations" array.

Follow platform character limits, exclude competitor brand names from any iOS keywords, and map top-scoring keywords to the highest-impact metadata locations. If you make assumptions (e.g., baseline CVR for sample size), state them in the respective file. Keep all file paths exactly as specified.
